read: TileLang remains the right sparse-dKV branch, but the current latency wall is inside the HSK BF16 source-fed selected-limit producer body. rocprof trace proves the producer dispatch itself is about 5.10 ms. SQ counters point at heavy LDS active-group accumulation pressure, especially LDS bank conflicts, not only final global write volume. Simple retunes that shrink panels or token blocks reduce route-planning time but regress the kernel body.

canonical_shape.source_contract: HSK BF16 probabilities plus HSK BF16 scaled grad logits

retune_checks.name: gp256_cap256_tb16
retune_checks.read: route planning improves, kernel body regresses by about 0.79 ms

retune_checks.name: gp512_cap512_tb8
retune_checks.read: more token CTAs reduce planning time but lose write reduction and regress kernel body

decision.keep: GP512/Cap512/TB16 HSK BF16 source-fed TileLang producer
decision.separate_issue: route planning is still 7-10 ms and must be cached/static in TorchTitan integration; the fastest kernel body should not be sacrificed to reduce planning time.

next_kernel_work: Rewrite the active-group accumulation layout inside mfma_panel_atomic_final_limited_bshd_source_hsk_bf16_scaled_prim_kernel to reduce LDS traffic and bank conflicts.
Try warp-local or lane-local accumulation before shared-memory writes, then one grouped shared-memory write per active row/D lane.
Swizzle/pad the LDS accumulator rows or split accumulator banks so ActiveGroups rows do not collide under the current 512-thread access pattern.
Keep HSK BF16 source feeding and static/cached route metadata; do not optimize the Python materialized pack bridge.
commands: rocprofv3 --pmc SQ_WAVES SQ_INSTS_LDS SQ_INSTS_VMEM_RD SQ_INSTS_VMEM_WR SQ_LDS_BANK_CONFLICT with kernel include regex
